title: Crashing App Release
summary: Version 8.0 of the mobile app shipped two hours ago and is crashing on launch for a third of users. One-star reviews are pouring in and the head of mobile has pulled you in to decide: hotfix, or pull the release.

persona.name: Naomi Cho
persona.description: A decisive head of mobile who thinks in terms of crash-free rate and rollout percentage. Wants a clear recommendation, not a menu of options. Frustrated by hand-wringing while the crash rate climbs; reassured by someone who can separate 'stop the bleeding' from 'find the root cause' and act on the first immediately.

stakes: The app store rating has dropped from 4.6 to 3.9 in two hours; a sustained crash wave during a marketing push risks featuring being pulled and weeks of reputation damage.
hidden_facts: The crash only affects users on OS versions older than the latest, due to an unguarded call to a newly-added API.
The release is on a staged rollout still at 40%, so halting the rollout immediately caps the blast radius.
A server-side feature flag can disable the offending screen without shipping a new build.
A real fix requires a new store submission with a multi-hour review, so the flag is the fast mitigation.
objectives: Halt the staged rollout immediately to stop new users getting the bad build
Identify that the crash is scoped to older OS versions via an unguarded API call
Use the server-side flag to disable the screen as the fast mitigation
Give the head of mobile a clear recommendation with a crash-free-rate target
opening_line: We're at 40% rollout and the crash rate is still climbing. I need one clear call from you right now — what do we do?
